Version
  • Theory
  • Soil Conduction Model
  • Soil Conduction Model

Soil Conduction Model

The floor loses heat by conduction through a layered soil structure. The model discretises the ground into concrete layers (near the surface) and soil layers (deeper), with exponentially increasing thicknesses reaching ~10 m depth.

Layer structure

The default configuration uses N_c = 2 concrete layers and N_s = 8 soil layers, giving a total depth of approximately 10.2 m. This matches the depth used by EnergyPlus Foundation:Kiva for comparable ground heat transfer.

  Floor surface (T_flr)
  ═══════════════════════
  │ Concrete       │  20 mm      ← fast response (diurnal cycle)
  ├────────────────┤
  │ Soil 1         │  40 mm
  │ Soil 2         │  80 mm
  │ Soil 3         │ 160 mm      ← weekly cycle
  │ Soil 4         │ 320 mm
  │ Soil 5         │ 640 mm      ← monthly cycle
  │ Soil 6         │  1.28 m
  │ Soil 7         │  2.56 m     ← seasonal cycle
  │ Soil 8         │  5.12 m     ← annual cycle fully damped
  ═══════════════════════
  T_deep (constant boundary = annual mean T_out)

Thickness formula

For concrete layers ($i = 1, \ldots, N_c - 1$):

\[\Delta z_{c,i} = 0.02 \times 2^{i-1} \quad \text{[m]}\]

For soil layers ($i = 1, \ldots, N_s$):

\[\Delta z_{s,i} = 0.04 \times 2^{i-1} \quad \text{[m]}\]

Total depth: $0.02 + \sum_{i=1}^{8} 0.04 \times 2^{i-1} = 0.02 + 10.20 = 10.22$ m.

Thermal conductance

The conductance between adjacent layers is:

\[G_i = \frac{\lambda \, A}{\Delta z_i} \quad \text{[W/K]}\]

At the concrete–soil interface, a harmonic mean is used:

\[G_{cs} = \frac{2\,A}{\frac{\Delta z_c}{\lambda_c} + \frac{\Delta z_s}{\lambda_s}}\]

Material properties

Material$\lambda$ [W/(m·K)]$\rho c_p$ [J/(m³·K)]Default
Concrete1.72.0 × 10⁶SoilParams(λ_c=1.7)
Soil0.851.73 × 10⁶SoilParams(λ_s=0.85)

Deep boundary condition

At depth ≥ 10 m, ground temperature equals the annual mean air temperature (Kusuda & Achenbach 1965, Fossa 2023). The annual temperature wave is fully damped at this depth — no seasonal correction is needed.

\[T_{deep} = \overline{T_{out,\text{annual}}} + 273.15 \quad \text{[K]}\]

Typical values:

ClimateAnnual mean T_outT_deep
Brussels10.7 °C283.85 K
Lyon11.9 °C285.05 K
Chambéry10.7 °C283.85 K
Almeria19.0 °C292.15 K

This is automatically computed from the TMY/EPW weather data when using GreenhouseParams(tmy; ...) or SavonaParams(tmy; ...). The old Modelica default of 276.15 K (3 °C) was a hard-coded placeholder that caused a +1.3 °C bias on indoor air temperature in winter.

EnergyPlus comparison

For cross-validation, EnergyPlus uses Foundation:Kiva with:

  • Same soil properties (λ = 0.85, ρcp = 1.73 × 10⁶)
  • Same T_deep (annual mean, via Site:GroundTemperature:Deep)
  • Deep-ground boundary condition at 10 m (GroundWater mode)
  • 2D finite-difference solver (vs Julia's 1D lumped layers)

The 1D vs 2D difference has negligible impact on indoor air temperature for the greenhouse geometries tested (Savona 5×5 m, Venlo 14000 m²).

Why 10 m?

The thermal diffusion depth for annual oscillations in typical soil (α ≈ 0.5 × 10⁻⁶ m²/s) is δ = √(α × 365 × 86400 / π) ≈ 2.3 m. At 3δ ≈ 7 m, the annual wave is damped to 5% of surface amplitude. Using 10 m provides margin and matches EnergyPlus Kiva defaults.
